executive_summary: Global systemic stability remains moderate despite localised volatility in Middle Eastern energy markets. The marked divergence between elevated Taiwanese media panic and zero physical ground truth suggests information warfare rather than an imminent kinetic conflict. Steady global fuel reserves mitigate immediate supply chain vulnerabilities to keep overall systemic stress within manageable limits.
correlations: The volatile Middle East energy spike acts as a primary catalyst for the elevated supply chain stress score though its systemic transmission is currently buffered by stable global fuel reserves.
